directions.name: Trim PTX Export Scratch On The Restored Baseline
directions.hypothesis: The 128x128 two-stage sibling recovered tensor activity, but the remaining gap now looks like export-path overhead: the PTX hot-band export scratch and row/quad store helpers still carry avoidable shared-memory and address-calculation cost. Reducing that footprint should cut DRAM/L2 traffic without reopening the closed promotion families.
directions.expected_bottleneck: PTX epilogue export bandwidth, shared scratch lifetime, and L2 traffic in the hot-band store path.
directions.risk: Low to medium. The change surface is narrow and still inside the restored PTX baseline family, but the upside is capped if the current slowdown is mostly environmental.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the next run does not beat 27.07665539 ms or if DRAM/L2 throughput does not fall alongside the export cleanup.

directions.name: One More Bounded 128x128 Two-Stage Sibling Iteration
directions.hypothesis: The 128x128 two-stage sibling is the only active distinct kernel family that already showed a large gain over the closed K32 staged path. A bounded feed/sync tuning in its cp.async and shared-memory handoff may recover the last bit of lost runtime without reopening default promotion or changing tile shape.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization and feed latency inside the 128x128 two-stage kernel, not tensor throughput.
directions.risk: Medium. The family already moved the profile in the right direction, but it may still be fundamentally limited by the same memory/export pressure as the restored baseline.
directions.stop_condition: If the next measurement stays above the restored PTX baseline or the stall mix does not improve, close this sibling family and move to a cleanup-only direction.

directions.name: Tighten PTX Hot-Band Grouping And Peel Orchestration
directions.hypothesis: The remaining gap may live in the hot-band to peeled-row handoff rather than the main MMA loop. A narrowly bounded orchestration tweak around the PTX grouped-row mapping or the peel launch boundary could reduce redundant coordination cost without reviving the closed broad-default family.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band orchestration overhead and peel coordination, especially around grouped-row partitioning.
directions.risk: Medium to high. This is a narrow change, but if the slowdown is dominated by memory/export traffic it may not move the needle.
directions.stop_condition: Abandon this family if the profile remains essentially unchanged or if the tweak pulls the kernel back toward the closed default-promotion behavior.

notes: Round 80/100 diagnosis for run 20260420_114650_bf16_gemm_v1_1b91857. The broad default-dispatch promotion family remains closed-negative, and the K32 staged family is also closed-negative after the round-79 regression. The existing 128x128 two-stage sibling improved strongly from 31.55292797 ms to 27.07665539 ms, with tensor activity back near baseline at 48.18 and long scoreboard down at 1.51, but it still trails the restored PTX baseline in the current environment while dram (31.39) and LTS (27.25) remain elevated. Treat the remaining gap as export/orchestration work, not another baseline-restore loop.
